rationale: Empirically probed commit 68fe576e against R3/R4/R5 with 30 scratch Go tests (whitebox, real Load() over /tmp config fixtures, deleted after run; git status clean). Per-field inheritance chain: reviewers and approve_threshold resolve independently through adhoc->bead->global->default at every combination (bead-only, adhoc-own-override, adhoc-reviewers-only inheriting bead's threshold, adhoc-threshold-only inheriting bead's reviewers, bead-threshold-only inheriting global reviewers) -- all correct, including the two-hop adhoc->bead(absent)->global chain walk. Threshold-range refusals verified at every link: own-gate out-of-range, global integer inherited by a differently-sized gate, bead integer inherited through adhoc, boundary values (exactly at sum, at 1, sum+1 refused), and n-1 valid at any size. One of my own test cases initially 'failed' by asserting a load should succeed when it actually hit R4(f)/(g)'s cross-field refusal (bead's threshold, valid for bead's own sum, invalid once inherited by a smaller reviewers-only adhoc) -- on inspection this is exactly the refusal spec R4/AC3 mandates, so it confirms correct behavior, not a defect. Substitutes edges: self-map refused, both empty-key and empty-value refused, mutual pair (A->B,B->A) loads fine, a chain-shaped map (a->b,b->c) loads fine with no transitive resolution attempted, claude_sub_on_quota coexists inertly. The full standing-protocol YAML round-trips exactly: 9/9/6/12 reviewer counts, bead's explicit lenses preserved verbatim, adhoc unconfigured produces byte-identical slots to bead's resolved slots, note is validation/resolver-inert across two otherwise-identical loads, substitutes and claude_sub_on_quota both parse. Unknown model ids never error; KnownModels() seed list matches the plan exactly; count-less entries default to 1; gates:{} behaves identically to absent. No deviation from R3/R4/R5 found in any of these 30 cases.
findings: CONFIRMED: per-field inheritance (reviewers, approve_threshold) walks adhoc->bead->global->default independently and correctly in all tested combinations, including two-hop chains where bead is entirely unconfigured.

CONFIRMED: R4(f)/(g) cross-field inheritance threshold check fires correctly for every named scenario in the spec text -- own-gate out-of-range, global-inherited-by-smaller-gate, and bead-inherited-through-adhoc-by-smaller-reviewers-only-adhoc -- at exact boundary values (threshold==sum passes, threshold==sum+1 refused, threshold==1 always passes).

CONFIRMED: substitutes validation refuses self-mapping and either empty side, accepts a legal mutual pair, and does not attempt any transitive/chain resolution (a->b,b->c loads without complaint, consistent with 'one-step resolution only').

CONFIRMED: the Goal's full standing-protocol YAML (four gates + note + substitutes + explicit lenses) round-trips through Load with every field equal to what was written; adhoc-follows-bead identity holds byte-for-byte at the slot level; panel.note is fully validation- and resolver-inert.

CONFIRMED: KnownModels() returns exactly the six seeded ids in the documented order; an unknown model id never causes Load to fail; count-less reviewer entries default to CountValue()==1; gates:{} is behaviorally identical to an absent gates: key across all five gate keys.

No defects found within this lens's scope (empirical black-box probing via the exported Config API). Did not probe cursor/lens-expansion adversarial cases (F1's lens) or type-level/schema internals (S2's lens) beyond what surfaced incidentally.
